Title: TC-001: Basic Map Stability (Primary Bug Fix)
Preconditions:
- PR #2138 deployed to test environment
- Test URL: https://virtostart-demo-store.govirto.com/cart
- Shopping cart contains at least 2 products
- Multiple pickup locations configured
Steps:
1. Add products to cart
2. Navigate to cart page
3. Scroll to shipping section
4. Select "Pickup" option
5. Click "Select pickup location"
6. Measure map container dimensions (note height/width)
7. Enter location name in search field
8. Verify map dimensions remain unchanged
9. Verify map does not jump/shift position
10. Clear search field using X button
11. Verify map dimensions remain unchanged
12. Verify map does not jump/shift position
13. Enter address in search field
14. Clear search field manually (backspace)
15. Verify map dimensions remain unchanged
16. Repeat steps 7-15 three times
Expected Result:
- Cart page displays correctly with items
- Pickup location selector opens on Pickup selection
- Map modal opens with map displayed
- Map container maintains consistent dimensions throughout all operations
- No visual "jumping" or layout shifts occur
- Smooth transitions between states
- Search field accepts input and clears properly
- Consistent behavior across multiple iterations

Title: TC-002: Desktop View - Location Selection Flow
Preconditions:
- Desktop browser (1920x1080 or similar)
- PR #2138 deployed
- Test URL accessible
- Cart has products
- Multiple pickup locations available
Steps:
1. Open pickup location modal
2. Verify desktop layout (map on right, list on left)
3. Click on a location in the list
4. Verify location highlights on map
5. Verify map pans smoothly to selected location
6. Verify info window opens on marker
7. Click different location in list
8. Verify previous highlight clears, new location highlights
9. Verify map animates smoothly to new location
10. Click marker on map directly
11. Verify info window opens
12. Click "Select" button in info window
13. Verify PickupLocationCard dialog opens
14. Verify card content (location details, availability, contact info)
15. Click "Confirm" button
16. Verify modal closes and pickup location is set in cart
Expected Result:
- Modal opens with proper desktop layout
- Location list visible on left, map on right
- Clicking list items highlights markers and pans map smoothly
- Info windows open correctly on markers
- Only one location highlighted at a time
- PickupLocationCard displays with all required information
- Confirmation flow completes successfully
- Selected location appears in cart page
- All interactions smooth without lag
- No race conditions in info window display

Title: TC-011: Performance Testing
Preconditions:
- Test environments with varying location counts
- Browser dev tools for performance monitoring
- Network throttling capability
Steps:
Test with 1-5 locations:
1. Measure modal open time (should be instant)
2. Measure search response time
3. Measure filter response time
4. Verify 60fps smooth interactions

Test with 10-50 locations:
5. Measure modal open time (should be <1s)
6. Test search performance
7. Test filter performance
8. Verify smooth map pan/zoom

Test with 50-100 locations:
9. Measure modal open time (should be <2s)
10. Verify acceptable performance
11. Check memory usage (dev tools)
12. Monitor network requests

Test with 100+ locations:
13. Verify map clustering or pagination
14. Check for memory leaks
15. Verify reasonable API call count
Expected Result:
Performance targets:
- 1-5 locations: Instant load, immediate interactions
- 10-50 locations: <1s load, smooth 60fps interactions
- 50-100 locations: <2s load, acceptable smooth interaction
- 100+ locations: Map clustering active or pagination
- No memory leaks detected
- Reasonable API call count (not excessive)
- Smooth 60fps for pan/zoom at all counts
- Search/filter response <500ms
- Modal opens within acceptable timeframes

Title: TC-012: Error Handling & Edge Cases
Preconditions:
- Ability to simulate various error conditions
- Test data with edge cases
- Network throttling tools
Steps:
1. Open modal with zero locations configured
2. Verify appropriate empty state message
3. Block Google Maps API (simulate failure)
4. Verify graceful fallback or error message
5. Deny browser geolocation permission
6. Verify app continues without geolocation
7. Test location with invalid coordinates
8. Verify error handled, no crash
9. Disconnect network during search
10. Verify appropriate error message shown
11. Throttle network to slow 3G
12. Verify loading states display
13. Test location with 200+ character name
14. Verify text truncated or wrapped appropriately
15. Test location with special characters/emojis
16. Verify displays correctly
17. Simulate concurrent user selections (if possible)
18. Verify no conflicts
Expected Result:
- Empty state handled gracefully with clear message
- Map API failure shows fallback or error (no blank crash)
- Geolocation denial does not block functionality
- Invalid coordinates handled without crash
- Network errors show clear error messages
- Slow connection shows loading indicators
- Long names truncated/wrapped without breaking layout
- Special characters and emojis display correctly
- No crashes in any error scenario
- User can recover from all error states
- Error messages are clear and actionable
- Graceful degradation in all scenarios

Preconditions:

- Latest browser versions
- Mobile devices or emulators for Safari iOS and Chrome Android
Steps:
For Chrome (Desktop & Android):
1. Run TC-001 (Map Stability)
2. Run TC-002 (Desktop Flow)
3. Run TC-003 (Mobile Toggle)
4. Run TC-004 (Search)
5. Run TC-006 (Map Interactions)
6. Run TC-007 (Location Card)
7. Verify all features work correctly

For Firefox (Desktop):
8. Repeat steps 1, 2, 4, 6, 7

For Safari (Desktop & iOS):
9. Repeat steps 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7

For Edge (Desktop):
10. Repeat steps 1, 2, 4, 6, 7

Compare appearance and behavior across all browsers
Expected Result:
- All functionality works in Chrome (desktop & mobile)
- All functionality works in Firefox
- All functionality works in Safari (desktop & iOS)
- All functionality works in Edge
- No browser-specific bugs
- Consistent appearance across browsers (minor differences acceptable)
- Map loads and works in all browsers
- Animations smooth in all browsers
- No console errors in any browser
- Touch events work on mobile browsers
- Performance acceptable in all browsers

14. Verify form fields have proper labels

Title: BUG-001: Pickup Map Width After Search (VCST-4518)
Preconditions:
- PR #2138 deployed to test environment
- Test URL: https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com
- Cart contains at least one product
- Multiple pickup locations configured
Steps:
1. Add product to cart and navigate to cart page
2. Select "Pickup" under SHIPPING DETAILS
3. Click "Select pickup address" button
4. Verify "Pick points" modal opens with list (left) and map (right)
5. Measure initial map width - verify approximately 50% of modal width
6. Enter search term "Westent foxpost" in search field
7. Click Search button
8. Verify "Pickup points not found" message displays
9. **CRITICAL CHECK:** Verify map maintains at least 40-50% of modal width (not ~30% or less)
10. Verify map remains usable and visible
11. Click "Reset search" button
12. Verify map returns to normal width (~50%)
13. Enter search term "South Schuylershire" in search field
14. Click Search button
15. Verify one location result displays
16. **CRITICAL CHECK:** Verify map maintains at least 40-50% of modal width with valid results
17. Click on "South Schuylershire" location in list
18. Verify "Pick point info" card opens
19. **CRITICAL CHECK:** Verify info card has sufficient space and readable layout
20. Verify map width remains stable throughout all operations
Expected Result:
**EXPECTED (AFTER FIX):**
- Initial state: Map occupies ~50% of modal width
- After empty search: Map maintains at least 40-50% width
- After valid search: Map maintains at least 40-50% width
- After reset: Map returns to ~50% width
- Info card: Opens with sufficient space, readable content
- Map never becomes unusably narrow (<40% width)
- List/empty state capped at reasonable width (max 350-400px)

**BUG BEHAVIOR (BEFORE FIX - VCST-4518):**
- Initial: ✅ Map OK (~50% width)
- After "Westent foxpost": ❌ Map narrow (~30% width)
- After reset: ✅ Map restored (~50% width)
- After "South Schuylershire": ❌ Map narrow (~30% width)
- Info card: ❌ Layout constrained, overlays narrow map
- Root cause: CSS layout - list takes excessive width, map lacks min-width
- Evidence: Reports/BUG-Pickup-Map-*.png (5 screenshots)
- Jira: VCST-4518 Comment ID 87266
